title: simplicio.quality-matrix/v1 (aka the #283 Quality Gate receipt)
description: Fail-closed evidence receipt read by simplicio_loop/quality_matrix.py::evaluate_quality_matrix. Produced as <run_dir>/quality-matrix.json and consumed by the completion oracle (simplicio_loop/oracle.py) before any work item can be reported COMPLETE. A missing or malformed receipt NEVER evaluates to ready=true.

run_id.description: optional: the run this receipt belongs to, for cross-linking with the delivery/evidence receipts and the task anchor

work_item.description: optional provenance of the source work item this receipt was produced for

coverage_threshold.description: minimum measured coverage percentage required; malformed/out-of-range values fail closed rather than falling back to the default

policy.description: #283: opt-in toggles. Omitting this object entirely reproduces the exact #278 behavior (every REQUIRED_REQUIREMENTS lane mandatory, no TDD lane evaluated, no lane excusable as NOT_APPLICABLE).

tdd_required.description: when true, the 'tdd' requirement (see below) is evaluated and must pass with DISTINCT red_proof_ref/green_proof_ref

allow_justified_not_applicable.description: when true, the 'benchmark' lane (the only lane in NOT_APPLICABLE_ELIGIBLE) may be excused by setting its status to not_applicable AND supplying a non-empty justification. No other lane is ever excusable this way, regardless of this flag.

requirements.description: one entry per mandatory lane; 'implementation', 'unit', 'integration', 'system', 'regression', 'benchmark' are always read (unless waived via policy.<lane>_required=false), 'tdd' is read only when policy.tdd_required is true

measured.description: the actually-measured coverage percentage (e.g. from scripts/coverage_gate.py); null/non-numeric fails closed as coverage_unmeasured

report_ref.description: #283: optional pointer to the raw coverage-gate report (scripts/quality_matrix.py populate writes <run_dir>/coverage-gate-report.json here) backing `measured` -- not required by evaluate_quality_matrix, but read by an independent re-verifier that wants to cross-check the number against its source artifact

proof_ref.description: pointer to the evidence, e.g. a test id, receipt path, or CI run URL; empty proof_ref on a 'pass' status is rejected as unproven

justification.description: required, non-empty, when status=not_applicable and policy.allow_justified_not_applicable is true

red_proof_ref.description: evidence the test FAILED before the implementation (RED)

green_proof_ref.description: evidence the SAME test PASSED after the implementation (GREEN); must differ from red_proof_ref
